channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Members of burned LA church bear a cross through their devastated neighborhood to mark Good Friday
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Carrying a tall wooden cross on his shoulder, Pastor John Shaver walked down the street where his church had stood, just three months ago.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: MISSING: 59-year-old man out of Taylorsville
item.description: The Dept. of Public Safety issued a Utah Endangered Missing Advisory Friday night for a Taylorsville man.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, ABC 4

item.title: A prince and knight fall in love and the Supreme Court is asked to intervene
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A prince lassos a dragon, saving a knight in shining armor from certain death. But the prince slips and as he falls, the knight and his steed race to return the favor. Then the two men fall in love.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tunisian opposition figures sentenced to up to 66 years in prison
item.description: TUNIS, Tunisia (AP) — A court in Tunisia has sentenced leading opposition figures to between 13 and 66 years in prison in the climax of a major trial accusing 40 people of conspiring against state security, according to Tunisian media reports.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Takeaways from AP’s report on pardoned Jan. 6 rioters being embraced in Republican politics
item.description: JACKSON, Mich. (AP) — Those who rioted, assaulted police officers or broke into congressional offices during the riot at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021 are now being received as honored guest speakers at local Republican events around the country.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: America's housing shortage by the numbers
item.description: The U.S. has been short of the homes it needs to meet its growing population for more than a decade, driving up prices and shooting down the dreams of millions of would-be buyers.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Spring allergies are back. Here's how to check pollen levels and keep from sneezing
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Allergy season can be miserable for tens of millions of Americans when trees, grass, and other pollens cause runny noses, itchy eyes, coughing and sneezing.
item.dc:creator: KENYA HUNTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Menendez brothers could get freedom under California law signed by Gavin Newsom: expert
item.description: A California trial lawyer breaks down an analysis of what the judge in the Menendez brothers resentencing hearings is considering when factoring in a potential prison release.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Orange Crush 2025: Georgia vacation town braces for mayhem as crime-filled beach fest returns
item.description: Orange Crush is expected to draw tens of thousands of people to Tybee Island, Georgia, this Easter weekend. Event organizers promise to keep the event safe and clean.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration makes major cuts to Native American boarding school research projects
item.description: At least $1.6 million in federal funds for projects meant to capture and digitize stories of the systemic abuse of generations of Indigenous children in boarding schools at the hands of the U.S. government have been slashed due to federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ten Commandments, ‘In God We Trust’ in classrooms is now Arkansas law
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders signed a bill into law this week making religious displays in classrooms state law.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian drummer arrested on child sexual abuse material charges in California
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Joseph Seiders, drummer for Canadian indie rock band The New Pornographers, has been arrested and charged with possessing child sex abuse materials, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 67% of CEOs surveyed disapprove of Trump's tariffs
item.description: A survey of more than 300 American business owners and CEOs revealed that 76% of the group believe tariffs will hurt their companies.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump moves to invoke Schedule F to make it easier to fire some federal workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump has begun making one of the controversial personnel changes for government employees that was spelled out in the conservative Project 2025 blueprint for his second term.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps Mark Levin, Henry McMaster to ‘revamped’ Homeland Security council
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that he has picked Fox News contributor Mark Levin, South Carolina Gov. Henry McMaster (R) and other conservative allies to serve on a "revamped" Homeland Security Advisory Council (HSAC).
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 10,000 pages of records about Robert F. Kennedy's 1968 assassination are released, on Trump's order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — About 10,000 pages of records related to the 1968 assassination of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy were released Friday, including handwritten notes by the gunman, who said the Democratic presidential candidate “must be disposed of” and acknowledged an obsession with killing him.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK and HAYA PANJWANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ACLU asks Supreme Court to block ‘imminent’ wave of Alien Enemies Act deportations
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union (ACLU) asked the Supreme Court on Friday to immediately intervene to block the Trump administration from deporting Venezuelan migrants being held in Texas to El Salvador under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trader Joe's is planning to open 21 new locations, including 5 in California: Here's where
item.description: Is your neighborhood lacking a grocery store that could easily double a well-lit tiki bar? That may change in the coming year.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawsuit challenges Trump administration crackdown on international students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A class action lawsuit filed Friday asks a federal court to reinstate the legal status of international students caught up in a Trump administration crackdown that has left more than a thousand fearful of deportation.
item.dc:creator: ALIA WONG, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Deliberations to resume Monday at bribery trial of Nadine Menendez, wife of ex-US Sen. Bob Menendez
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A jury will resume deliberations Monday in the bribery case brought against Nadine Menendez after a prosecutor told a jury that she was a “critical participant” in a five-year fraud with her husband, former U.S. Sen. Bob Menendez.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge to allow 'bushy eyebrows' testimony in Bryan Kohberger's quadruple murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — A judge says a former roommate of four University of Idaho students who were killed in 2022 can testify about seeing an intruder with “bushy eyebrows” around the time of the crime.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apartments are getting larger -- but maybe not where you live
item.description: RentCafe’s annual report on apartment living shows some communities are seeing apartment square footage increase for the first time in years. But maybe not yours.
item.dc:creator: Peter Wilgoren, KTLA 5

item.title: Fox News True Crime Newsletter: Menendez brothers' move, Alex Murdaugh's accomplice, Luigi Mangione's charges
item.description: Stay up to date with the Fox News True Crime Newsletter, which brings you the latest cases ripped from the headlines, from crime to courts, legal and scandal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montana reports first measles cases since 1990
item.description: Two children have died amid the outbreak this year and a third death is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, KRON4

item.title: US has 800 measles cases and outbreaks in several states. Here's what you should know
item.description: The U.S. has 800 cases of measles nationwide as of Friday, and two more states identified outbreaks this week. Texas is driving the high numbers, with an outbreak centered in West Texas that started nearly three months ago and is up to 597 cases.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Video shows doctor with measles treating kids. RFK Jr later praised him as an ‘extraordinary’ healer
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — A Texas doctor who has been treating children in a measles outbreak was shown on video with a measles rash on his face in a clinic a week before Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. met him and praised him as an “extraordinary” healer. Dr.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE R. SMITH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Nintendo announces new date for Switch 2 pre-orders
item.description: Nintendo announced Friday a new date for Nintendo Switch 2 pre-orders, which were initially supposed to start on April 9 but were pushed back due to President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs. Pre-orders for the new console will officially begin on April 24, and it will launch nationwide on June 5.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Supreme Court blocks, for now, new deportations under 18th century wartime law
item.description: The Supreme Court has blocked, for now, the deportations of any Venezuelans held in northern Texas under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ryan Gosling to star in next 'Star Wars' film, Lucasfilm announces
item.description: The Force was with many Japanese, as well as visitors from abroad, at a “Star Wars” event on Friday where Lucasfilm announced the next installation in the film franchise.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, ABC 4

item.title: 2 food service employees killed in FSU shooting
item.description: A food service worker was identified as one of two people killed when a gunman opened fire on the Florida State University campus Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Military officials identify 2 Marines killed in crash during border deployment
item.description: SANTA TERESA, N.M. (AP) — Military officials have identified the two Marines from California who were killed when their vehicle crashed as a convoy was traveling along the U.S.-Mexico border near Santa Teresa, New Mexico. They were Lance Cpl. Albert Aguilera, 22, of Riverside and Lance Cpl.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, ABC 4
